en.extract:
The little green sunbird (Anthreptes seimundi), also called Seimund's sunbird, is a species of bird in the family Nectariniidae. It is sometimes placed in the genus Nectarinia. It is widespread throughout the African tropical rainforest.


Description
The little green sunbird is small, light yellow, and warbler-like; overall similar to the Bates's sunbird. It is 9 cm (about 3.5 inches) long. The beak is slightly curved and has a small amount of pink. Around the eye is a pale ring. Its cry is high pitched.


Name
The little green sunbird's binomial classification Anthreptes seimundi comes from its genus and Eibert Carl Henry Seimund, a British Taxidermist.


Subspecies
A. s. seimundi: Bioko (Gulf of Guinea)
A. s. kruensis: Guinea and Sierra Leone to Ghana and Togo.
A. s. minor: southern Nigeria and southern Cameroon east to Central African Republic, southern South Sudan, Uganda, and Rwanda, south to northern Angola and central Democratic Republic of the Congo.

It is 9 cm (about 3.5 inches) long. The beak is slightly curved and has a small amount of pink. Around the eye is a pale ring. Its cry is high pitched. == Name == The little green sunbird's binomial classification ''Anthreptes seimundi'' comes from its genus and Eibert Carl Henry Seimund, a British [[Taxidermist]].{{Cite journal |last1=Cheke |first1=Robert |last2=Mann |first2=Clive |date=2020 |title=Little Green Sunbird (Anthreptes seimundi), version 1.0 |url=https://birdsoftheworld.org/bow/species/ligsun2/cur/introduction |journal=Birds of the World |language=en |doi=10.2173/bow.ligsun2.01|s2cid=216404388 |url-access=subscription }} ==Subspecies== *''A. s. seimundi'': [[Bioko]] (Gulf of Guinea) *''A. s. kruensis'': Guinea and Sierra Leone to Ghana and Togo. *''A. s. minor'': southern Nigeria and southern Cameroon east to Central African Republic, southern South Sudan, Uganda, and Rwanda, south to northern Angola and central Democratic Republic of the Congo. ==Gallery== Little green sunbird (Anthreptes seimundi kruensis) composite.jpg|Little green sunbird (''A. s. kruensis'') composite ==References== {{Reflist}} {{Taxonbar|from=Q1930587}} [[Category:Anthreptes|little green sunbird]] [[Category:Birds of the African tropical rainforest]] [[Category:Birds described in 1908|little green sunbird]] [[Category:Taxonomy articles created by Polbot]] {{Nectariniidae-stub}}
